{"step_index":33,"source":"MODEL","type":"PLANNER_RESPONSE","status":"DONE","created_at":"2026-05-13T13:52:04Z","content":"[ROUTING: Direct — reason: Answering questions, planning, conversation]\n\nThe `context deadline exceeded` error happens because the MCP client (your extension/agent environment) expects the MCP server to initialize and respond within a very short default timeout (usually 5 to 10 seconds). \n\nFor servers using `npx` (like `google-ads`, `gsc`, `magic`), the initialization can take longer because `npx` needs to fetch or verify packages. For `stitch-mcp`, it takes extra time to verify authentication against Google Cloud before fully exposing the stdio bridge. When the startup process takes longer than the default timeout, the connection gets killed.\n\nI have updated your `~/.gemini/settings.json` and `~/.gemini/antigravity/mcp_config.json` files to include a `\"timeout\": 60` setting for all of your MCP servers. This extends the initialization window to 60 seconds.\n\n**To apply the fix:**\nPlease **reload your VS Code / IDE window** (or restart the Gemini extension) so it picks up the new timeout configuration.\n\n<truncated 307 bytes>"}
